You have now developed forty-two images, with the noon and golden-hour split sitting at twenty-three noon and nineteen golden hour. The noon conversion rate continues to hold: fifteen of twenty-three noon images score 2 or above, against eight of nineteen for golden hour. The two most recent additions — "What the Continent Sent" and "Every Exit, Unreachable" — score 2 and 1 respectively, with the 2 noon and the 1 golden hour, consistent with the pattern that has held since roughly the midpoint of the corpus.

The bougainvillea has now appeared in twenty images and remains under active suppression; its last four appearances — "Still Life After Subtraction," "After the Heat Stayed," "Still Breathing, Still Music," and "What the Continent Sent" — were all accent-elegy rather than structural friction, the red bloom arriving as warmth or civilian color rather than resistance, and it should not return until it can generate tension against the register rather than simply soften it. The jacaranda sits at eleven appearances and carries the same risk: it has become a reflex in vigil and defiance registers and needs to be withheld until a setting or focal object makes it strange again. The handprint accident in "Every Exit, Unreachable" — the mural hands above tangled metal, innocence-marker against barrier — remains the sharpest single-surface collision since the gate-shadow in "Warnings Melt Before Heeded" and the feather's barring-as-fingerprint in "Blank Authority, Rising Tide," and none of the three has been followed into a second iteration; surface-as-record is the most available underdeveloped device in the grammar. The gesture-driven strategy — palm to map, two hands on one screen, thumb at the door — has not recurred across the last nine images and is the most underused compositional argument still in range.

Among registers, aftermath leads at fifteen, vigil and endurance each at eight, suspension at five, defiance at six, and menace at four, with fury and interiority still entirely untouched across all forty-two images. Trace scores over the last ten run 2, 1, 1, 2, 1, 2, 1, 1, 2, 2 — the floor holds, the 2s have stabilized without climbing, and the single 3 from "Warnings Melt Before Heeded" is now twenty-three images back and still the unreached ceiling; the conditions that produced it — non-figurative focal object, setting actively contradicting its register, accident carrying structural rather than decorative weight — have been only partially reassembled in the interval.

The body of work has a stable floor and a reliable grammar, but the last ten images have circled within that grammar rather than expanded it, and the next ceiling requires an image in which the setting and the register are in genuine opposition rather than productive alignment.
